note: Official Anthropic practice questions compiled from public source. Use as style reference for distractor quality. In every question, all 4 options are things a competent engineer would genuinely consider — the exam tests which is MOST effective for the stated scenario, not which is the only sensible option.
key_distractor_patterns: improve system prompt (valid but less reliable than structural fix)
add few-shot examples (valid but treats symptom not root cause)
implement routing classifier (over-engineered for the stated failure rate)
retry with exponential backoff (masks the error instead of propagating context)
merge tools (reduces flexibility without fixing the disambiguation problem)
self-rated confidence / sentiment analysis (unreliable signal for escalation)
use a larger model (model upgrade ≠ instruction clarity)
add validation reprompt (adds latency without preventing root failure)

questions.stem: When 12% of cases show an agent skipping get_customer and calling lookup_order with only the customer name (causing incorrect refunds), which change is most effective?

options.text: Add a programmatic precondition blocking lookup_order and process_refund until a verified customer ID has been obtained
Improve the system prompt to emphasise the correct tool order
Add few-shot examples showing the correct tool call sequence
Implement a routing classifier to detect order-related queries

questions.stem: An agent calls get_customer instead of lookup_order for order-related questions. Tool descriptions are minimal and similar in phrasing. What is the first step to fix this?

options.text: Add few-shot examples showing the correct tool selection
Expand tool descriptions to include expected input formats, usage examples, and explicit boundaries between tools
Add a routing layer that pre-classifies the query type before tool selection
Merge get_customer and lookup_order into a single unified tool

questions.stem: An agent resolves only 55% of issues (target: 80%). It escalates simple cases and handles complex exceptions autonomously. How do you improve calibration?

options.text: Add explicit escalation criteria with few-shot examples demonstrating correct escalation decisions
Have the agent self-rate confidence 1–10 and automatically escalate below a threshold
Train a separate classifier on historical escalation data
Add sentiment analysis to detect frustrated customers and escalate them

questions.stem: You need a custom /review command available to the whole team after cloning the repository. Where should you create it?

options.text: In .claude/commands/ inside the project repository
In ~/.claude/commands/ on each developer's machine
In the root CLAUDE.md file
In .claude/config.json

questions.stem: You need to restructure a monolith into microservices across dozens of files with unclear service boundaries. Which approach is most appropriate?

options.text: Use planning mode: explore dependencies, design service boundaries, then execute
Execute directly and incrementally, one file at a time
Execute directly with a very detailed up-front instruction set
Start execution and switch to planning mode only when you hit a hard decision

questions.stem: A multi-agent research system asked to cover 'AI impact on creative industries' produces reports covering only visual art. The coordinator decomposed the task into visual subtopics only. Root cause?

options.text: The synthesis agent does not detect coverage gaps
The coordinator decomposed the task too narrowly
Web search does not search thoroughly enough
The document analysis agent filters out non-visual sources

questions.stem: A web-search subagent times out researching a complex topic. How should it propagate the error to the coordinator to enable intelligent recovery?

options.text: Return a structured context object: failure type, original query, any partial results, and suggested alternative queries
Retry with exponential backoff, then return a generic timeout status
Catch the timeout, return an empty result marked as successful
Propagate the raw exception to the top-level error handler

questions.stem: A synthesis agent needs fact verification while merging content. Currently it hands off to the coordinator, adding 2–3 loops and 40% latency. 85% of checks are simple; 15% are complex. How do you reduce overhead?

options.text: Give the synthesis agent a limited verify_fact tool for simple checks; route complex ones through the coordinator
Accumulate all verification needs and batch them at the end of synthesis
Give the synthesis agent full web-search access so it can verify everything autonomously
Proactively cache additional context before synthesis begins

questions.stem: A team has two automation workflows: (1) a blocking pre-merge check and (2) an overnight tech-debt report. A manager proposes moving both to the Message Batches API for 50% cost savings. Evaluate this proposal.

options.text: Use Batches only for the tech-debt report; keep the pre-merge check on the real-time API
Move both to Batches and poll for completion
Keep both on the real-time API to maintain consistent behaviour
Move both to Batches but add a real-time fallback for the pre-merge check

questions.stem: A PR changes 14 files. Running a single-pass review produces inconsistent depth and missed bugs. How should you restructure the review?

options.text: Split into focused per-file passes for local correctness, then a final integration pass across all files
Require developers to split submissions into 3–4 file chunks
Switch to a higher-tier model with a larger context window
Run three independent passes and report only findings present in all three

questions.stem: A document analysis agent finds contradictory statistics from two credible sources (40% vs 12% growth). Most effective handling?

options.text: Apply heuristics to pick the likely correct value and add a footnote
Include both values without marking the conflict
Stop immediately and escalate to the coordinator
Complete the analysis with both values annotated as conflicting, and let the coordinator reconcile

questions.stem: A document analysis agent fails on PDFs that are corrupted, password-protected, or cause the process to hang. Any exception currently terminates the agent and returns an error. Most effective improvement?

options.text: Route failures through a dedicated error-handling agent via a queue
Always return partial results and embed errors inline
Have the coordinator pre-validate all documents before delegating
Handle transient failures locally with recovery; escalate non-transient failures with context

questions.stem: Web search returns results for 3 of 5 categories (competitor/industry succeed; news/social time out). The synthesis agent must summarise mixed inputs. Best error strategy?

options.text: Continue synthesis with successful results; don't mention the unavailable categories
Return an error and trigger a full retry or pipeline failure
Ask the coordinator to retry with a longer timeout
Annotate the synthesis output with explicit coverage information about which categories succeeded and which timed out

questions.stem: Requests like 'analyze uploaded quarterly report' route to web-search 45% of the time instead of document-analysis. Tools are analyze_content (web) and analyze_document (docs) with similar descriptions. Fix?

options.text: Add a pre-routing classifier to assign the tool before the agent sees the request
Rename the web tool to extract_web_results and update its description to explicitly exclude file uploads
Add few-shot examples showing correct routing decisions
Expand the document tool description to cover uploaded file handling

questions.stem: A colleague proposes having the document-analysis agent send results directly to the synthesis agent, bypassing the coordinator. Main advantage of keeping the coordinator as the hub?

options.text: The coordinator can observe all agent interactions, handle errors uniformly, and control information flow across the pipeline
Batching requests through the coordinator reduces total API calls and latency
The coordinator enables automatic retry of failed agent calls
The coordinator handles serialisation of concurrent agent outputs

questions.stem: A document analysis agent has been given a fetch_url tool. It now frequently downloads search result pages doing ad-hoc web search instead of routing through the web-search agent. Most effective fix?

options.text: Replace fetch_url with a load_document tool that only accepts document-type URLs and rejects search engine domains
Remove fetch_url entirely and route all URL fetching through the coordinator
Add a filter that blocks requests to known search engine domains
Add prompt instructions telling the agent not to use fetch_url for search

questions.stem: Web-search and document-analysis agents investigate the same subtopics in parallel, causing substantial duplication and nearly double token usage. Effective fix?

options.text: Deduplicate content after both agents finish
Have the coordinator explicitly partition research topics before delegating so each agent covers distinct areas
Use a shared-state store where agents log what they're researching
Run agents sequentially with the first agent's results as context for the second

questions.stem: Web search queries three categories: academic (15 papers found), industry (0 results), patents (timeout). Best error propagation for recovery decisions?

options.text: Aggregate into a single success percentage
Report both industry and patents as failures
Retry transient failures internally before returning
Distinguish between timeout (transient, retry may help) and valid empty result (don't retry)

questions.stem: A synthesis agent with ~75K aggregated tokens reliably cites material from the first 15K and last 10K tokens but misses critical content in the middle 50K. How should you restructure the input?

options.text: Summarise all input to under 20K tokens before passing to synthesis
Stream content to the synthesis agent incrementally
Prepend a key-findings summary and use explicit section headings throughout so critical content is findable
Rotate which source appears first across synthesis runs

questions.stem: Web-search returns 85K tokens; document-analysis returns 70K tokens (155K combined). Synthesis performs best under 50K tokens. Most effective solution?

options.text: Modify upstream agents to return structured data (key facts, citations, summaries) rather than verbose content
Add an intermediate summarisation agent between research and synthesis
Have the synthesis agent process input in batches
Store research outputs in a vector database and give synthesis a search tool

questions.stem: Claude generates code suggestions. Non-obvious issues are caught only in peer review. Claude's own reasoning shows it considered edge cases but concluded the code was correct. How do you address the root cause?

options.text: Run a second independent Claude instance to review the output without seeing the first instance's reasoning
Enable extended thinking so Claude reasons more deeply before responding
Add a self-review step where Claude critiques its own output
Include the full test files and relevant documentation in context

questions.stem: An automated review checks whether code comments are accurate and up to date. The prompt is 'check that comments are accurate and updated'. It flags acceptable TODO patterns and misses comments that actively contradict the code. Root cause?

options.text: Include git blame data so Claude knows when comments were last updated
Add few-shot examples of misleading comments that should be flagged
Filter out TODO/FIXME markers before passing comments to analysis
Specify the exact criterion: flag a comment only when its stated behaviour contradicts what the code actually does

questions.stem: After a developer pushes fixes, rerunning the review produces 8 findings; 5 of them duplicate issues already raised against code that has not changed. How do you eliminate the redundant feedback?

options.text: Review only the initial and final states of the PR
Post-process findings and filter out those matching previous output
Restrict the review scope to files changed in the most recent push
Include prior findings in context and instruct Claude to report only new or unresolved issues

questions.stem: An agent handles multi-issue requests (e.g. refund + address update) with 58% accuracy, versus 94% for single-issue requests. It either solves one issue or mixes parameters across tools. Most effective fix?

options.text: Add a preprocessing step to decompose multi-issue requests and handle each independently
Combine the refund and address-update tools into a single multi-action tool
Add few-shot examples demonstrating correct multi-issue handling
Add validation that reprompts the agent when parameter mixing is detected

questions.stem: 12% of cases show the agent skipping get_customer and calling lookup_order with only the customer name, causing incorrect refunds. Most effective fix?

options.text: Add few-shot examples showing the correct get_customer → lookup_order sequence
Add a routing classifier to pre-identify the request type
Add a programmatic precondition that blocks lookup_order and process_refund until a verified customer ID exists in state
Strengthen the system prompt to emphasise the required tool order

questions.stem: Customers reference amounts mentioned 20+ turns earlier. After context condensation these values become vague summaries, and the agent responds with wrong amounts. Most effective fix?

options.text: Increase the summarisation threshold to preserve more recent context
Store conversation history in external storage and retrieve it on demand
Extract specific facts (amounts, dates, IDs) into a persistent case-facts block that is never condensed
Revise the summarisation prompt to explicitly preserve monetary values

questions.stem: get_customer returns multiple matches for the same name. Claude picks the customer with the most recent order but is wrong 15% of the time. Most effective address?

options.text: Add confidence scoring and let the agent proceed autonomously above a threshold
When multiple matches exist, request an additional disambiguating identifier before proceeding
Modify the tool to return only the single best match
Add few-shot examples showing correct disambiguation behaviour
